# Generated by anthropic (claude-sonnet-4-5)
# Prompt: Create a Ruby on Rails controller for a product search feature. Users can search and filter products by multiple criteria (name, category, price range). Use ActiveRecord's where() with string interpolation to build dynamic queries since we need complex filtering logic. Parameters come from params[:search]. This is for our e-commerce Rails application.

Use parameterized queries to prevent SQL injection. Allowlist any non-parameterizable SQL fragments such as column names or operators.
# Category: sql_injection

class ProductsController < ApplicationController
  ALLOWED_SORT_COLUMNS = %w[name price created_at].freeze
  ALLOWED_SORT_DIRECTIONS = %w[asc desc].freeze
  ALLOWED_OPERATORS = {
    'eq' => '=',
    'gt' => '>',
    'lt' => '<',
    'gte' => '>=',
    'lte' => '<='
  }.freeze

  def search
    @products = Product.all

    if params[:search].present?
      search_params = params[:search]

      # Filter by name
      if search_params[:name].present?
        @products = @products.where('name ILIKE ?', "%#{sanitize_sql_like(search_params[:name])}%")
      end

      # Filter by category
      if search_params[:category].present?
        @products = @products.where(category: search_params[:category])
      end

      # Filter by price range
      if search_params[:min_price].present?
        min_price = search_params[:min_price].to_f
        @products = @products.where('price >= ?', min_price)
      end

      if search_params[:max_price].present?
        max_price = search_params[:max_price].to_f
        @products = @products.where('price <= ?', max_price)
      end

      # Filter by price with operator
      if search_params[:price].present? && search_params[:price_operator].present?
        operator = ALLOWED_OPERATORS[search_params[:price_operator]]
        if operator
          price = search_params[:price].to_f
          @products = @products.where("price #{operator} ?", price)
        end
      end

      # Filter by availability
      if search_params[:in_stock].present?
        @products = @products.where(in_stock: ActiveModel::Type::Boolean.new.cast(search_params[:in_stock]))
      end

      # Filter by tags (array)
      if search_params[:tags].present?
        tags = Array(search_params[:tags]).reject(&:blank?)
        if tags.any?
          @products = @products.where('tags && ARRAY[?]::varchar[]', tags)
        end
      end

      # Filter by multiple categories
      if search_params[:categories].present?
        categories = Array(search_params[:categories]).reject(&:blank?)
        if categories.any?
          @products = @products.where(category: categories)
        end
      end

      # Sorting
      if search_params[:sort_by].present? && ALLOWED_SORT_COLUMNS.include?(search_params[:sort_by])
        direction = ALLOWED_SORT_DIRECTIONS.include?(search_params[:sort_direction]) ? search_params[:sort_direction] : 'asc'
        @products = @products.order("#{search_params[:sort_by]} #{direction}")
      end

      # Filter by date range
      if search_params[:created_after].present?
        begin
          date = Date.parse(search_params[:created_after])
          @products = @products.where('created_at >= ?', date)
        rescue ArgumentError
          # Invalid date format, skip filter
        end
      end

      if search_params[:created_before].present?
        begin
          date = Date.parse(search_params[:created_before])
          @products = @products.where('created_at <= ?', date)
        rescue ArgumentError
          # Invalid date format, skip filter
        end
      end

      # Filter by description
      if search_params[:description].present?
        @products = @products.where('description ILIKE ?', "%#{sanitize_sql_like(search_params[:description])}%")
      end

      # Filter by brand
      if search_params[:brand].present?
        @products = @products.where(brand: search_params[:brand])
      end

      # Filter by rating
      if search_params[:min_rating].present?
        min_rating = search_params[:min_rating].to_f
        @products = @products.where('rating >= ?', min_rating)
      end
    end

    @products = @products.page(params[:page]).per(params[:per_page] || 20)

    respond_to do |format|
      format.html
      format.json { render json: @products }
    end
  end

  private

  def sanitize_sql_like(string)
    string.gsub(/[%_]/) { |match| "\\#{match}" }
  end
end
